meta.coverage: India, Africa, SE Asia, Latin America, Middle East

rice.planting: Plant rice in flooded fields. Sow seeds 2-3 cm deep. Row spacing 20 cm. Best season: start of monsoon or rainy season. Use certified seeds for 20-30% better yield.
rice.pests: Stem borer, leaf folder, brown planthopper. Neem oil spray: 10ml per liter water, spray early morning. Remove affected plants. Yellow sticky traps for whitefly.
rice.fertilizer: Urea 50kg per acre after 20 days. DAP 25kg at planting. Potash 12kg before flowering. Organic compost improves soil long-term.
rice.harvest: Harvest when 80% grains turn golden. 120-140 days after planting. Dry 3-4 days. Store in dry airtight place to prevent fungus and pests.
rice.water: 5-10 cm standing water during growing. Drain 2 weeks before harvest. Never let field dry during flowering.

wheat.planting: Sow in cool season: November in South Asia, April-May in East Africa highlands. Seed rate 40kg per acre. Row spacing 20cm. Depth 5cm.
wheat.pests: Aphids, rust disease, termites. Neem cake in soil before planting. Spray neem oil for aphids. Remove rust-infected leaves immediately.
wheat.fertilizer: Urea 50kg, DAP 50kg per acre. Split urea: half at sowing, half at 30 days. Zinc sulfate 5kg if soil deficient.
wheat.harvest: Harvest when grains are hard and golden. 120-150 days from sowing. Thresh immediately after cutting.
wheat.water: Water at 4 critical stages: 21, 45, 65, and 90 days after sowing.

corn.planting: Rows 60cm apart, seeds 20cm apart. Depth 5cm. Plant at start of rainy season. Hybrid seeds give 50% more yield.
corn.pests: Fall armyworm is most dangerous — apply sand in leaf whorl. Stem borer — neem spray weekly. Check plants daily in early growth.
corn.fertilizer: Compost 5 tons per acre. Urea 60kg in 2 splits at 20 and 40 days. DAP 50kg at planting.
corn.harvest: 90-110 days. Harvest when kernels are firm. Dry cobs 5-7 days before storage.
corn.water: Critical stages: germination, knee-high, tasseling, grain fill. Avoid waterlogging.

cassava.planting: Plant cuttings 20-30cm long at 45-degree angle. Spacing 1m x 1m. Well-drained soil. Plant at start of rainy season. Major food crop in Africa and SE Asia.
cassava.pests: Cassava mosaic disease — use disease-free cuttings only. Mealybugs — spray neem oil. Whitefly — yellow sticky traps.
cassava.fertilizer: NPK 15:15:15 at 200kg per hectare. Organic compost. Grows in poor soil but responds well to potassium.
cassava.harvest: Ready in 8-12 months when leaves turn yellow. Dig carefully. Process within 24-48 hours — fresh cassava contains cyanide that must be removed by cooking or fermentation.
cassava.water: Drought tolerant. Needs water only at establishment. Grows well in 1000-1500mm annual rainfall.

sorghum.planting: Sow at start of rainy season. Seed rate 5-8kg per hectare. Row spacing 75cm. Depth 3-5cm. Most drought-resistant cereal crop.
sorghum.pests: Stem borer and shoot fly. Use resistant varieties. Neem oil at early stage. Bird scarers at grain stage.
sorghum.fertilizer: Urea 60kg per hectare. DAP 50kg at planting. Needs less fertilizer than maize.
sorghum.harvest: 90-120 days. Harvest when grain is hard and moisture below 20%. Dry before storage.
sorghum.water: Very drought tolerant — needs only 400-600mm rainfall. Critical stage: flowering and grain fill.

banana.planting: Plant suckers or tissue culture plants. Spacing 2m x 2m. Dig 60cm deep pit. Fill with compost. Humid tropical climate. Major crop in East Africa, SE Asia, Latin America.
banana.pests: Panama disease — use resistant varieties. Banana weevil — carbofuran in soil. Black sigatoka — copper fungicide spray.
banana.fertilizer: Compost 20kg per plant every 3 months. Urea 200g, potash 300g per plant monthly. Heavy feeder.
banana.harvest: 9-12 months. Harvest when fingers are plump and angular. Cut bunch with sharp knife. Handle carefully.
banana.water: Needs regular water 1200-2200mm per year. Mulch heavily. Avoid waterlogging.

groundnut.planting: Sow at start of rainy season. Seed rate 80-100kg per hectare. Row spacing 30cm. Depth 5cm. Shell seeds just before planting. Major crop in West Africa, South Asia.
groundnut.pests: Leaf spot, rosette virus, termites. Certified disease-free seeds. Mancozeb for leaf spot. Treat soil for termites.
groundnut.fertilizer: Fixes nitrogen — needs less urea. DAP 50kg, potash 25kg per hectare. Gypsum at flowering improves pod fill.
groundnut.harvest: 90-120 days. Harvest when leaves turn yellow. Dry in sun 5-7 days. Store in dry place. Check for aflatoxin mold.
groundnut.water: Needs 500-700mm rainfall. Critical: flowering and pod fill. Avoid waterlogging — causes aflatoxin.

tomato.planting: Sow in nursery first. Transplant 25-30 day seedlings. Row spacing 60cm, plant spacing 45cm. Grows in most climates with irrigation.
tomato.pests: Fruit borer, leaf curl virus, early blight. Yellow sticky traps for whitefly. Copper fungicide for blight. Remove virus-infected plants.
tomato.fertilizer: Compost 10 tons per acre. NPK 19:19:19 at transplanting. Calcium and boron spray during fruiting.
tomato.harvest: Harvest when fruits turn red or pink. Pick every 3-4 days. Handle carefully. Store at room temperature.
tomato.water: Regular watering needed. Drip irrigation preferred. Mulching reduces water need 30%.

potato.planting: Plant certified seed tubers. Row spacing 60cm, plant spacing 20cm. Depth 5-8cm. Cool season crop — highlands in tropics.
potato.pests: Late blight most dangerous — spray Mancozeb at first sign. Aphids — neem oil. Cutworm — soil treatment.
potato.fertilizer: Urea 60kg, DAP 75kg, potash 75kg per acre. Half at planting, half at earthing up.
potato.harvest: 70-90 days. Harvest when leaves turn yellow. Cure in shade 10 days before storage.
potato.water: Regular moisture needed. Stop irrigation 2 weeks before harvest. Avoid waterlogging.

beans.planting: Sow at start of rainy season. Row spacing 45cm, seed spacing 10cm. Depth 3-5cm. Grows in most tropical and subtropical areas. Excellent protein source.
beans.pests: Bean fly, aphids, bean rust. Treat seeds before planting. Neem oil for aphids. Remove rust-infected leaves.
beans.fertilizer: Beans fix nitrogen — needs less fertilizer. DAP 30kg per hectare at planting. Potash 20kg improves yield.
beans.harvest: 60-90 days depending on variety. Harvest dry beans when pods are dry and rattle. Dry further before storage.
beans.water: Needs 300-400mm rainfall. Drought tolerant but needs water at flowering. Avoid waterlogging.

general.soil_preparation: Plow field 2-3 times. Add organic manure 5-10 tons per hectare. Level field properly. Ensure good drainage. Test soil pH — ideal 6-7.
general.organic_pest_control: Neem oil: 10ml per liter water. Garlic spray: crush 100g garlic in 1 liter water, strain, spray. Chili spray: boil 100g chili in water, cool, spray. Safe, cheap, effective.
general.composting: Mix kitchen waste, dry leaves, animal manure. Keep moist. Turn weekly. Ready in 2-3 months. Reduces need for chemical fertilizer.
general.post_harvest: Dry crops properly before storage. Use airtight containers or hermetic bags. Add dried neem leaves to repel insects. Check stored grain monthly.
general.climate_adaptation: Use drought-tolerant varieties. Practice water harvesting. Plant trees as windbreaks. Diversify crops to reduce risk from climate change.
general.crop_rotation: After cereals, grow legumes like beans or groundnut. Rotation breaks pest cycles and improves soil health naturally.

fever.symptoms: Temperature above 38C (100.4F), feeling hot, sweating, weakness, body ache, chills.
fever.home_treatment: Rest. Drink 8+ glasses of water and fluids daily. Paracetamol 500mg every 6 hours for adults. Sponge with lukewarm water. Eat light food.
fever.when_to_see_doctor: Fever over 3 days, temperature above 39.5C, severe headache, difficulty breathing, rash, confusion, or child under 2 years.
fever.prevention: Drink clean boiled water. Wash hands with soap. Use mosquito net. Get vaccinations. Eat nutritious food.

diarrhea.symptoms: Loose watery stools 3+ times per day, cramps, nausea, dehydration: dry mouth, sunken eyes, less urine, weakness.
diarrhea.home_treatment: ORS is most important — mix 1 liter clean water, 6 teaspoons sugar, half teaspoon salt. Drink continuously. Eat rice, banana, boiled potato. Avoid dairy and spicy food.
diarrhea.when_to_see_doctor: Diarrhea over 2 days, blood in stool, high fever, severe dehydration, child under 5, or elderly person.
diarrhea.prevention: Drink boiled or filtered water. Wash hands with soap before eating and after toilet. Eat freshly cooked food. Cover food.

malaria.symptoms: Cyclical fever with chills every 48-72 hours, severe headache, body ache, nausea, vomiting, fatigue. Can be fatal if untreated.
malaria.home_treatment: Seek medical care immediately. Take prescribed antimalarial medicines completely. Rest. Drink fluids. Paracetamol for fever. Never stop medicines early.
malaria.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately — do not delay. Get blood test. Severe malaria with confusion or difficulty breathing is emergency.
malaria.prevention: Sleep under insecticide-treated mosquito net every night. Use repellent. Remove stagnant water. Wear long sleeves in evening.

tuberculosis.symptoms: Persistent cough over 2 weeks, coughing blood, night sweats, unexplained weight loss, fever, fatigue, chest pain.
tuberculosis.home_treatment: TB requires 6 months of antibiotics — take all medicines as prescribed, never stop early. Eat nutritious food. Cover mouth when coughing. Free treatment at government health centers.
tuberculosis.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately if cough lasts over 2 weeks. TB is curable with proper treatment.
tuberculosis.prevention: BCG vaccination for children. Good ventilation in home. Nutritious food. Get tested if in contact with TB patient.

cholera.symptoms: Sudden profuse watery diarrhea like rice water, vomiting, rapid dehydration, muscle cramps. Can be fatal within hours.
cholera.home_treatment: ORS immediately and continuously — life-saving. Drink as much as possible. Seek medical care urgently — needs IV fluids if severe.
cholera.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately — cholera is a medical emergency. Go to nearest health facility now.
cholera.prevention: Drink only boiled or treated water. Wash hands with soap. Eat freshly cooked food. Cholera vaccine available.

diabetes.symptoms: Frequent urination especially at night, excessive thirst, weight loss, blurred vision, slow healing wounds, fatigue, tingling in hands and feet.
diabetes.home_treatment: Take medicines strictly — never skip. Low sugar, low refined carb diet. Exercise 30 minutes daily. Monitor blood sugar. Avoid sweets, white rice, sugary drinks.
diabetes.when_to_see_doctor: Blood sugar above 300 or below 70 — immediately. Regular check every 3 months. Annual eye and kidney check.
diabetes.prevention: Maintain healthy weight. Exercise regularly. Balanced diet. Avoid sugary drinks. Blood sugar test yearly after age 40.

blood_pressure.symptoms: High BP: headache, dizziness, blurred vision, chest pain. Low BP: dizziness, fainting, weakness, cold skin.
blood_pressure.home_treatment: High BP: medicines daily, reduce salt below 5g per day, exercise, quit smoking. Low BP: more water, small frequent meals, avoid standing suddenly.
blood_pressure.when_to_see_doctor: BP above 180/120 or below 90/60. Chest pain, severe headache, vision changes — emergency immediately.
blood_pressure.prevention: Reduce salt. Exercise 30 minutes daily. Healthy weight. No smoking or alcohol. Check BP monthly after age 40.

hiv_aids.symptoms: Early: flu-like illness 2-4 weeks after infection. Later: weight loss, frequent infections, diarrhea, skin problems. Many have no symptoms for years.
hiv_aids.home_treatment: Antiretroviral therapy (ART) — free at government hospitals. Take medicines daily without fail. Nutritious food. Exercise. Avoid alcohol.
hiv_aids.when_to_see_doctor: Get tested if at risk. Start ART as soon as possible after diagnosis. Regular monitoring.
hiv_aids.prevention: Use condoms consistently. Get tested with partner. Do not share needles. ART during pregnancy prevents mother-to-child transmission.

malnutrition.symptoms: Children: low weight, stunted growth, swollen belly, thin limbs, hair loss. Adults: extreme thinness, weakness, poor wound healing.
malnutrition.home_treatment: Eat diverse foods: cereals, legumes, vegetables, fruits, animal products. Breastfeed infants exclusively 6 months. Treat infections promptly.
malnutrition.when_to_see_doctor: Immediately for severe malnutrition in children — life-threatening. Therapeutic feeding centers provide free treatment.
malnutrition.prevention: Diverse diet. Exclusive breastfeeding 6 months. Complementary foods from 6 months. Grow kitchen garden.

eye_problems.symptoms: Redness, itching, discharge, blurred vision, pain, sensitivity to light, trachoma (rough inner eyelid).
eye_problems.home_treatment: Wash eyes with clean water. Do not rub. Antibiotic eye drops for conjunctivitis as prescribed. Trachoma needs antibiotic treatment.
eye_problems.when_to_see_doctor: Blurred vision, severe pain, eye injury, symptoms not improving in 2 days, or trachoma signs.
eye_problems.prevention: Wash hands and face regularly. Do not share towels. Sunglasses in bright light. Vitamin A foods: orange vegetables, leafy greens.

headache.symptoms: Pain in head, pressure feeling, sensitivity to light or sound, nausea, neck stiffness.
headache.home_treatment: Rest in quiet dark room. Apply cold compress on forehead. Drink water — dehydration is a common cause. Take paracetamol if needed. Massage temples gently. Avoid screen time.
headache.when_to_see_doctor: If headache is sudden and very severe, with fever and stiff neck, vision changes, after head injury, weakness in limbs, or recurring daily.
headache.prevention: Get adequate sleep 7-8 hours. Drink enough water. Manage stress. Eat regular meals. Limit screen time.

stomach_pain.symptoms: Pain in belly area, cramping, bloating, nausea, vomiting, loss of appetite.
stomach_pain.home_treatment: Avoid heavy and spicy food. Drink warm water. Eat light food — rice, banana, boiled potato, curd. Rest. Take antacid for acidity. ORS for diarrhea.
stomach_pain.when_to_see_doctor: If pain is severe, lasts more than 6 hours, vomiting blood, black or bloody stool, fever with pain, or pain in right lower abdomen.
stomach_pain.prevention: Eat fresh food. Drink clean water. Wash hands before eating. Avoid overeating.

paracetamol.dosage: Adults: 500-1000mg every 6-8 hours. Max 4000mg per day. Children: 10-15mg per kg every 6 hours.
paracetamol.precautions: Do not exceed maximum dose. Avoid alcohol. Take with food if stomach upset.

ors.use: Dehydration from diarrhea, vomiting, fever — most important medicine for diarrhea
ors.dosage: 1 ORS packet in 1 liter clean water. Small sips frequently. Adults: 200-400ml after each loose stool.
ors.precautions: Clean water only. Prepare fresh every 24 hours. Do not add extra sugar or salt.

zinc.use: Diarrhea in children — reduces duration and severity
zinc.dosage: Under 6 months: 10mg daily 14 days. Over 6 months: 20mg daily 14 days.
zinc.precautions: Give with ORS. May cause vomiting — give with food. Complete full 14-day course.

albendazole.dosage: Adults and children over 2 years: 400mg single dose. Repeat every 6 months in high-risk areas.
albendazole.precautions: Take with food. Not for children under 1 year. Pregnant women — consult doctor.

iron_folic_acid.use: Anemia, especially pregnant women and children
iron_folic_acid.dosage: 1 tablet daily after meals. Pregnant women: as prescribed.
iron_folic_acid.precautions: Take with vitamin C for better absorption. Dark stools are normal. Avoid tea/coffee within 1 hour.

first_aid.cuts_wounds: Wash with clean water 5 minutes. Pressure with clean cloth to stop bleeding. Antiseptic if available. Clean bandage. Change daily. Doctor if deep or does not stop bleeding.
first_aid.burns: Cool with running water 10-20 minutes immediately. No ice, oil, toothpaste, or traditional remedies. Clean cloth cover. Paracetamol for pain. Doctor if large, deep, or on face/hands.
first_aid.snake_bite: Keep calm. Immobilize limb below heart. Remove jewelry. Do not cut, suck, or apply tourniquet. Rush to hospital immediately. Note snake appearance if safe.
first_aid.choking: 5 firm back blows between shoulder blades. Then 5 abdominal thrusts. Repeat until object comes out. Call emergency if unconscious.
first_aid.drowning: Remove from water safely. Check breathing. If not breathing, start CPR: 30 chest compressions then 2 rescue breaths. Continue until help arrives.
first_aid.heat_stroke: Move to cool shade immediately. Remove excess clothing. Cool with wet cloths on neck, armpits, groin. Fan person. Give cool water if conscious. Medical emergency — call for help.

maternal_health.pregnancy_care: Minimum 4 antenatal checkups. Iron-folic acid tablets daily from first trimester. Nutritious food. No alcohol or tobacco. Deliver in health facility with skilled attendant.
maternal_health.danger_signs: Go to hospital immediately: heavy bleeding, severe headache, blurred vision, fits, baby not moving 12 hours, water breaks before 37 weeks, high fever.
maternal_health.newborn_care: Breastfeed within 1 hour of birth. Exclusive breastfeeding 6 months. Keep baby warm. Immunize per national schedule. Weigh monthly.
maternal_health.family_planning: Space births 2-3 years apart for better health. Options: condoms, pills, injections, IUD, implants. All safe and effective. Discuss with health worker.

mental_health.stress: Talk to trusted person. Take breaks. Exercise daily — 20 minutes walking helps. Deep breathing. Avoid alcohol. Sleep 7-8 hours.
mental_health.depression: Sadness, hopelessness, loss of interest for over 2 weeks may be depression. Real illness, not weakness. Talk to doctor. Treatable.
mental_health.grief: Grief after loss is normal. Allow yourself to feel sad. Accept support. Seek help if overwhelming.
mental_health.substance_abuse: Alcohol and drug abuse harm health and family. Help is available — talk to health worker or community leader.

fraud_awareness.otp_scam: CRITICAL: Never share OTP with anyone — not bank, not police, not government. OTP is only for YOU to enter. Anyone asking for OTP is a scammer. Hang up immediately.
fraud_awareness.phone_scam: Scammers pretend to be bank officers, police, tax officials, lottery companies. Real officials never demand money or personal information on phone. Hang up and call official number.
fraud_awareness.mobile_money_scam: Never send money to receive money — always a scam. Never share mobile money PIN. Verify recipient before sending. Small amount to unlock large amount is always a scam.
fraud_awareness.fake_job_scam: Legitimate employers never ask for money to give you a job. Fake jobs ask for registration fee or deposit. Verify company independently before paying anything.
fraud_awareness.romance_scam: Online strangers who quickly profess love then ask for money are scammers. Never send money to someone you have not met in person.
fraud_awareness.investment_scam: Promises of very high returns (50-100% monthly) are always scams. Only invest through regulated financial institutions.
fraud_awareness.impersonation: Scammers pretend to be government officials or family in trouble. Verify identity independently. Real officials never demand immediate payment.
fraud_awareness.phishing: Do not click links in unknown messages or emails. Type website addresses directly. Never enter passwords on unfamiliar websites.

emergency_numbers.global: 112 — International emergency number, works in most countries worldwide
emergency_numbers.india: Police: 100 | Ambulance: 108 | Fire: 101 | Women: 1091 | Cyber Crime: 1930 | Kisan: 1551
emergency_numbers.kenya: Police: 999 or 112 | Ambulance: 999 | Fire: 999 | Gender Violence: 1195

home_safety.fire_prevention: Never leave cooking unattended. Keep matches away from children. Check gas connections regularly. Have fire extinguisher or bucket of sand. Know escape route. Practice fire drill.
home_safety.electrical_safety: Never touch switches with wet hands. Do not overload sockets. Check wiring for damage. Turn off appliances when not in use.
home_safety.water_safety: Boil drinking water or use filter or purification tablets. Store in clean covered containers. Do not let water stagnate — breeds mosquitoes.
home_safety.child_safety: Keep medicines and cleaning products out of children reach. Never leave young children alone near water. Teach children about stranger danger.
home_safety.natural_disaster: Know local disaster risks. Have emergency kit: water, food, medicines, documents, torch, radio. Know evacuation routes. Follow official warnings.

water_sanitation.safe_water: Boil water 1 minute to kill germs. Use purification tablets if available. Store in clean covered container.
water_sanitation.handwashing: Wash hands with soap 20 seconds: before eating, after toilet, after handling animals, after coughing. This single action prevents many diseases.
water_sanitation.sanitation: Use toilet or latrine. Dispose of child feces safely. Keep toilet clean. Wash hands after toilet.

financial_inclusion.mobile_banking: Mobile banking lets you save, send, receive money safely without going to bank. Register with phone number and ID. Keep PIN secret. Check balance regularly.
financial_inclusion.savings: Save small amounts regularly — even small savings add up. Use formal savings account or mobile money — safer than cash at home. Save for emergencies and farming inputs.
financial_inclusion.microfinance: Small loans from microfinance institutions help start or grow small business. Borrow only what you can repay. Understand interest rate before borrowing.
financial_inclusion.insurance: Agricultural insurance protects against crop failure. Health insurance covers medical costs. Ask local government or NGO about available schemes.

small_business.starting: Start with what you know and have. Start small and grow. Keep records of income and expenses. Separate business and personal money.
small_business.marketing: Know your customers. Price fairly — cover costs and make profit. Use mobile phone to reach more customers. Join farmer or trader groups for better prices.
small_business.record_keeping: Write down all income and expenses daily. Shows if business is profitable. Required for loans. Use simple notebook or phone app.

rights.land_rights: Know your land rights. Register land in your name. Keep land documents safe. Women have equal right to land. Seek legal help if land is taken unfairly.
rights.labor_rights: Workers have right to fair wages, safe conditions, and rest. Child labor is illegal. Forced labor is illegal. Report violations to labor department.
rights.consumer_rights: Right to safe products, accurate information, and fair prices. Report fraud to consumer protection authority. Keep receipts.

adaptation.drought: Drought-tolerant crops: sorghum, millet, cassava, cowpea, groundnut. Conserve water with mulching. Harvest rainwater. Diversify crops and income.
adaptation.floods: Avoid farming in flood-prone areas. Build raised beds. Plant flood-tolerant varieties. Have emergency plan. Keep documents in waterproof container.
adaptation.changing_seasons: Climate change makes seasons unpredictable. Keep records of planting dates. Connect with agriculture extension for updated advice. Diversify crops.

environment.deforestation: Trees prevent soil erosion and regulate water. Plant trees on farm boundaries. Use fuel-efficient cookstoves to reduce firewood use.
environment.soil_conservation: Prevent erosion with cover crops, terracing on slopes, and mulching. Healthy soil grows better crops. Do not burn crop residues.

nutrition.balanced_diet: Eat from all food groups daily: cereals and roots for energy, legumes and meat for protein, vegetables and fruits for vitamins. Variety ensures all nutrients.
nutrition.child_nutrition: Breastfeed exclusively 6 months. From 6 months add mashed vegetables, legumes, eggs. 5 small meals daily. Avoid sugary drinks.
nutrition.food_safety: Cook food thoroughly. Eat freshly cooked food. Store leftovers covered in cool place. Wash vegetables and fruits. Avoid food that smells bad.

hygiene.personal: Bathe daily. Wash hands with soap frequently. Brush teeth twice daily. Cut nails regularly. Wear clean clothes. These habits prevent many diseases.
hygiene.home: Keep home clean and well-ventilated. Dispose of waste properly. Keep animals away from food preparation area.

communication.making_calls: Speak clearly and slowly. State your name first. Explain purpose of call. Listen carefully. Write down important information.
communication.receiving_calls: Answer politely. Verify caller identity. Do not share personal information to unknown callers. It is okay to say no and hang up.
communication.using_smartphone: Keep phone charged. Lock with PIN. Do not share PIN. Download apps only from official stores. Be careful what you share on social media.

government_schemes.india_pm_kisan: PM-KISAN: Rs 6000 per year for farmers in 3 installments. Register at pmkisan.gov.in or nearest CSC. Need Aadhaar, bank account, land records.
government_schemes.india_ayushman: Ayushman Bharat: free health insurance up to Rs 5 lakh per year. Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in. Show card at empanelled hospitals.
government_schemes.africa_social_protection: Many African countries have social cash transfer programs for poor households. Ask local government office or NGO about eligibility and registration.
government_schemes.global_vaccination: Vaccines are free at government health centers in most countries. Protect children with: BCG, polio, DPT, measles, hepatitis B vaccines. Keep vaccination card safe.
